frameworks.name: connectome-based predictive modeling
frameworks.one_line: whole-brain functional connectivity used to predict individual behavioral variability

frameworks.name: mind-wandering / executive control
frameworks.one_line: behavioral variability framed as indirect index of attentional lapses in aging

core_question: Can whole-brain task and resting-state functional connectivity predict response time variability, an indirect marker of mind-wandering, in older adults?
key_assumptions: trial-to-trial RT variability indexes mind-wandering / attentional lapses
individual differences in connectivity generalize to behavioral prediction
task-embedded thought probes are valid but limited in older adults
manipulations: whole-brain network features vs canonical network subsets
key_findings: brain-based predictive models of RT variability can be derived from whole-brain task functional connectivity in aging
interpretations: whole-brain functional networks, not just canonical ones, carry information about attentional fluctuations in older adults
